dict.string: TrailGuard needs your location to share your position with your riding group and provide safety features like crash detection.
TrailGuard needs your location at all times to track your ride, share your position with your group, and trigger automatic safety alerts in the background.
TrailGuard needs your location to show your position on the map and share it with your riding group.
TrailGuard uses motion data to detect crashes and trigger automatic safety alerts.
TrailGuard uses local network and Bluetooth to connect your device directly with nearby riders for offline group tracking when there's no internet.
TrailGuard uses Bluetooth to connect directly with nearby riders for offline mesh networking, enabling group tracking and safety alerts even without an internet connection.
TrailGuard uses Bluetooth to broadcast your presence to nearby riders and enable direct device-to-device communication for group ride coordination.
TrailGuard uses your camera to scan QR codes for quickly joining ride groups and connecting with other TrailGuard users.
